authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife

sources.title: Cal. Code Regs. Tit. 14, § 7.50(b)(152) — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Tahoe Lake and tributaries)
sources.quotedText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout (B) Tahoe Lake within 300 feet of the mouth of its tributaries. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

sources.title: Cal. Code Regs. Tit. 14, § 7.50(a)(1) — General Provisions (waters listed in subsection (b) closed except during the open season shown)
sources.quotedText: Every body of water listed in subsection (b) is closed to all fishing except during the open season as shown. Unless otherwise provided, waters shown as open to trout fishing in subsection (b), are open to fishing for other species. Gear restrictions listed in this section apply to the take of all species of fish unless otherwise noted.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (Watson Creek not listed by name)
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: List of Lake Tahoe inflow streams — Wikipedia (Watson Creek, north shore)
sources.quotedText: from near Mount Watson and Watson Lake, flows into Lake Tahoe at Ridgewood, near Carnelian Bay, California

groups.humanSummary: Watson Creek is a north-shore Lake Tahoe tributary: it runs from near Mount Watson and Watson Lake southeast into Lake Tahoe at Ridgewood, near Carnelian Bay (Placer Co.). It is not listed under its own name, but as a Tahoe Lake tributary it is governed by the special Tahoe-tributary regulation §7.50(b)(152)(A): catch-and-release open season Sat. before Memorial Day–Sep. 30, artificial lures with barbless hooks only, 0 trout; closed the remainder of the year per §7.50(a)(1). The covered reach runs from Lake Tahoe upstream to the first lake (Watson Lake sits near the creek's headwaters, about 39.225, -120.138).
groups.verbatimText: (152) Tahoe Lake and tributaries (Placer and El Dorado Cos.). (A) Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout (B) Tahoe Lake within 300 feet of the mouth of its tributaries. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

parameters.note: Watson Creek is a tributary of Lake Tahoe (it flows southeast into Lake Tahoe at Ridgewood near Carnelian Bay, Placer Co.). It is not listed by its own name in §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), but it falls under the Lake Tahoe tributaries entry §7.50(b)(152)(A), which applies to 'Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake.' Per §7.50(a)(1), waters listed in subsection (b) are closed to all fishing except during the shown open season — so the creek is closed Oct. 1 through the Friday preceding the Saturday before Memorial Day. Date_spec encodes 'Saturday preceding Memorial Day' as the last Monday of May (Memorial Day) minus 2 days, since the resolver has no anchor_holiday implementation.

regulations.humanSummary: Open catch-and-release only, Saturday before Memorial Day through Sept. 30; closed the rest of the year (Lake Tahoe tributary special regulation).
regulations.verbatimText: Tahoe Lake tributaries upstream to the first lake. Sat. preceding Memorial Day through Sep. 30. Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used. 0 trout

regulations.humanSummary: Artificial lures with barbless hooks only during the open window; no bait.
regulations.verbatimText: Only artificial lures with barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license. Watson Creek is wholly within California (Placer Co.); no Nevada reciprocity applies to the creek.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
